description_long: This is a tall, cylindrical wooden utility pole commonly found in urban and suburban environments, such as along streets or in neighborhoods. The pole is vertically oriented and features a single horizontal crossarm near the top, supported by two diagonal braces that connect to the main shaft. The wood surface is weathered, displaying a natural brown color with visible grain and some greenish discoloration near the base, likely from ground contact or treatment. Metal hardware is visible at the ends of the crossarm, likely for attaching electrical or communication wires. The pole is robust and designed to support overhead utility lines, but does not feature any mounted transformers, streetlights, or identification tags. Its utilitarian design is typical for supporting power or telecommunication infrastructure.
description: A tall, weathered wooden utility pole with a single horizontal crossarm and metal hardware, designed for supporting overhead wires in urban settings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the cylindrical wooden pole with the crossarm edge-on and minimal visible hardware.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full width of the horizontal crossarm, diagonal braces, and metal hardware at the ends.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, with the crossarm again seen edge-on and the pole's surface texture visible.
description_view_3: Rear view mirroring the front, showing the crossarm, braces, and hardware from the opposite side.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: A single horizontal crossarm is mounted near the top of the pole, supported by two diagonal braces.
